query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- Lesson 6: Jeremiah and Lamentations
(690006, 'Jeremiah is often called:', 'multiple_choice', '["The royal prophet", "The weeping prophet", "The warrior prophet", "The silent prophet"]', 'The weeping prophet', 1),
(690006, 'Jeremiah prophesied the exile would last:', 'multiple_choice', '["40 years", "70 years", "100 years", "400 years"]', '70 years', 2),
(690006, 'The New Covenant prophecy is found in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Jeremiah 1", "Jeremiah 7", "Jeremiah 31", "Jeremiah 52"]', 'Jeremiah 31', 3),
(690006, 'Lamentations is a collection of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Victory songs", "Funeral dirges over Jerusalem", "Wedding songs", "Temple hymns"]', 'Funeral dirges over Jerusalem', 4),
(690006, 'Jeremiah was forbidden by God to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Prophesy", "Marry", "Travel", "Eat meat"]', 'Marry', 5),
(690006, 'The potter and clay imagery in Jeremiah illustrates:', 'multiple_choice', '["Human creativity", "God''s sovereign right to shape nations", "Economic prosperity", "Artistic skill"]', 'God''s sovereign right to shape nations', 6),
(690006, 'Jeremiah was thrown into:', 'multiple_choice', '["A lion''s den", "A fiery furnace", "A cistern/pit", "Prison only"]', 'A cistern/pit', 7),
(690006, 'The New Covenant promises that God will write His law on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Stone tablets", "Scrolls", "Hearts", "Temple walls"]', 'Hearts', 8),
(690006, 'Lamentations 3:22-23 speaks of God''s:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wrath", "Mercies that are new every morning", "Judgment", "Silence"]', 'Mercies that are new every morning', 9),
(690006, 'Jeremiah purchased a field during the siege to symbolize:', 'multiple_choice', '["His wealth", "Future restoration", "His foolishness", "Land ownership"]', 'Future restoration', 10),
(690006, 'Explain how the New Covenant in Jeremiah 31 differs from the Mosaic Covenant and how it is fulfilled in Christ.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The New Covenant promises internal transformation with God''s law written on hearts, forgiveness of sins, and direct knowledge of God - fulfilled through Christ''s sacrifice.', 11),

-- Lesson 7: Ezekiel and Daniel
(690007, 'Ezekiel prophesied from:', 'multiple_choice', '["Jerusalem", "Babylon", "Egypt", "Assyria"]', 'Babylon', 1),
(690007, 'Ezekiel''s vision of dry bones symbolizes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Death without hope", "Israel''s restoration", "Judgment on enemies", "Temple destruction"]', 'Israel''s restoration', 2),
(690007, 'Daniel served in the courts of which empires?', 'multiple_choice', '["Egypt and Assyria", "Babylon and Persia", "Greece and Rome", "Israel and Judah"]', 'Babylon and Persia', 3),
(690007, 'Daniel''s three friends were thrown into:', 'multiple_choice', '["A lion''s den", "A fiery furnace", "A pit", "Prison"]', 'A fiery furnace', 4),
(690007, 'The "Son of Man" vision in Daniel 7 refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["An angel", "Daniel himself", "The Messiah", "A king of Babylon"]', 'The Messiah', 5),
(690007, 'Ezekiel''s vision of the new temple is found in chapters:', 'multiple_choice', '["1-10", "20-30", "40-48", "33-39"]', '40-48', 6),
(690007, 'The writing on the wall in Daniel 5 announced:', 'multiple_choice', '["Blessing on Babylon", "Babylon''s fall", "Daniel''s promotion", "A new king"]', 'Babylon''s fall', 7),
(690007, 'Ezekiel''s vision of God''s glory departing symbolized:', 'multiple_choice', '["God''s blessing", "God''s judgment and abandonment of the temple", "Victory over enemies", "Future prosperity"]', 'God''s judgment and abandonment of the temple', 8),
(690007, 'Daniel''s 70 weeks prophecy points to:', 'multiple_choice', '["The exodus", "The Messiah''s coming", "The exile", "The conquest"]', 'The Messiah''s coming', 9),
(690007, 'Daniel was thrown into the lion''s den because he:', 'multiple_choice', '["Committed a crime", "Refused to stop praying to God", "Insulted the king", "Failed in his duties"]', 'Refused to stop praying to God', 10),
(690007, 'Compare and contrast the apocalyptic visions in Daniel with their New Testament counterparts in Revelation.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Both use symbolic imagery of beasts, kingdoms, and divine judgment; Daniel''s visions provide the framework that Revelation expands upon regarding end times.', 11),

-- Lesson 8: Minor Prophets Part 1
(690008, 'The Minor Prophets are "minor" because:', 'multiple_choice', '["They are less important", "Their books are shorter", "They prophesied to minor nations", "They lived shorter lives"]', 'Their books are shorter', 1),
(690008, 'Hosea''s marriage to Gomer illustrated:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ideal marriage", "God''s faithful love despite Israel''s unfaithfulness", "The importance of wealth", "Political alliances"]', 'God''s faithful love despite Israel''s unfaithfulness', 2),
(690008, 'Joel prophesied about:', 'multiple_choice', '["A locust plague and the Day of the LORD", "The exodus", "David''s reign", "The temple construction"]', 'A locust plague and the Day of the LORD', 3),
(690008, 'Amos was a:', 'multiple_choice', '["Priest", "King", "Shepherd and fig farmer", "Scribe"]', 'Shepherd and fig farmer', 4),
(690008, 'Obadiah prophesied against:', 'multiple_choice', '["Israel", "Judah", "Edom", "Babylon"]', 'Edom', 5),
(690008, 'Jonah was sent to preach to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Jerusalem", "Samaria", "Nineveh", "Babylon"]', 'Nineveh', 6),
(690008, 'Micah 5:2 prophesies the Messiah''s birth in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Jerusalem", "Nazareth", "Bethlehem", "Egypt"]', 'Bethlehem', 7),
(690008, 'The book of Amos emphasizes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Temple rituals", "Social justice", "Political power", "Military conquest"]', 'Social justice', 8),
(690008, 'Joel''s prophecy about the Spirit being poured out was fulfilled at:', 'multiple_choice', '["The exodus", "The exile", "Pentecost", "The crucifixion"]', 'Pentecost', 9),
(690008, 'Jonah''s reluctance to preach to Nineveh reveals:', 'multiple_choice', '["His humility", "His nationalism and lack of compassion", "His wisdom", "His obedience"]', 'His nationalism and lack of compassion', 10),
(690008, 'How does the book of Jonah reveal God''s concern for all nations, not just Israel?', 'short_answer', NULL, 'God sent Jonah to pagan Nineveh, showing His desire for all peoples to repent and receive mercy, foreshadowing the gospel going to all nations.', 11),

-- Lesson 9: Minor Prophets Part 2
(690009, 'Nahum prophesied against:', 'multiple_choice', '["Jerusalem", "Babylon", "Nineveh", "Egypt"]', 'Nineveh', 1),
(690009, 'Habakkuk 2:4 states that "the righteous shall live by":', 'multiple_choice', '["Works", "Faith", "Law", "Sacrifice"]', 'Faith', 2),
(690009, 'Zephaniah prophesied during the reign of:', 'multiple_choice', '["David", "Solomon", "Josiah", "Nebuchadnezzar"]', 'Josiah', 3),
(690009, 'Haggai encouraged the people to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Flee to Egypt", "Rebuild the temple", "Prepare for war", "Fast and mourn"]', 'Rebuild the temple', 4),
(690009, 'Zechariah 9:9 prophesies the Messiah entering Jerusalem on:', 'multiple_choice', '["A horse", "A chariot", "A donkey", "Foot"]', 'A donkey', 5),
(690009, 'Malachi is the last book of:', 'multiple_choice', '["The Torah", "The Historical Books", "The Old Testament", "The Wisdom Literature"]', 'The Old Testament', 6),
(690009, 'Malachi prophesied about the coming of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Moses", "David", "Elijah", "Joshua"]', 'Elijah', 7),
(690009, 'Habakkuk questioned God about:', 'multiple_choice', '["Temple worship", "Why God would use wicked Babylon to judge Judah", "Marriage laws", "Sabbath observance"]', 'Why God would use wicked Babylon to judge Judah', 8),
(690009, 'Zechariah''s visions encouraged the post-exilic community with promises of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Immediate prosperity", "Restoration and God''s protection", "Military victory", "Political independence"]', 'Restoration and God''s protection', 9),
(690009, 'Malachi confronted the people about:', 'multiple_choice', '["Idol worship", "Corrupt worship and withholding tithes", "Political rebellion", "Foreign alliances"]', 'Corrupt worship and withholding tithes', 10),
(690009, 'How does Habakkuk 2:4 connect to New Testament theology, particularly in Paul''s letters?', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Paul quotes this verse in Romans and Galatians to establish that justification comes through faith, not works of the law.', 11),

-- Lesson 10: Old Testament Themes and Fulfillment
(690010, 'The major theme of covenant in the Old Testament finds fulfillment in:', 'multiple_choice', '["The Law of Moses", "The New Covenant in Christ", "The Davidic kingdom", "The temple"]', 'The New Covenant in Christ', 1),
(690010, 'The Passover lamb points forward to:', 'multiple_choice', '["The temple sacrifices", "Christ as our Passover", "The Day of Atonement", "The Feast of Tabernacles"]', 'Christ as our Passover', 2),
(690010, 'Jesus said He came not to abolish the Law but to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignore it", "Replace it", "Fulfill it", "Condemn it"]', 'Fulfill it', 3),
(690010, 'The temple theme finds ultimate fulfillment in:', 'multiple_choice', '["The rebuilt Jerusalem temple", "Christ as the temple and believers as God''s temple", "Heaven only", "Church buildings"]', 'Christ as the temple and believers as God''s temple', 4),
(690010, 'The promise to Abraham that "all nations will be blessed" is fulfilled through:', 'multiple_choice', '["Israel''s political dominance", "The gospel going to all nations through Christ", "Jewish missionaries", "The temple"]', 'The gospel going to all nations through Christ', 5),
(690010, 'The sacrificial system pointed to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ongoing animal sacrifices", "Christ''s once-for-all sacrifice", "Human works", "Priestly authority"]', 'Christ''s once-for-all sacrifice', 6),
(690010, 'David''s throne finds eternal fulfillment in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Solomon", "The Maccabees", "Jesus Christ", "Modern Israel"]', 'Jesus Christ', 7),
(690010, 'The exodus pattern of deliverance is fulfilled in Christ''s:', 'multiple_choice', '["Teaching ministry", "Deliverance from sin", "Political revolution", "Temple cleansing"]', 'Deliverance from sin', 8),
(690010, 'According to Luke 24:27, Jesus explained how all the Scriptures:', 'multiple_choice', '["Were outdated", "Pointed to Himself", "Were only for Israel", "Were symbolic only"]', 'Pointed to Himself', 9),
(690010, 'The Old Testament is essential for Christians because:', 'multiple_choice', '["It replaces the New Testament", "It provides the foundation for understanding Christ and salvation", "It is optional reading", "It only applies to Jews"]', 'It provides the foundation for understanding Christ and salvation', 10),
(690010, 'Synthesize how the major Old Testament themes of creation, covenant, exodus, temple, and kingdom all find their fulfillment in Jesus Christ.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Jesus is the agent of new creation, mediator of the New Covenant, deliverer from sin''s bondage, the true temple where God dwells, and the eternal King on David''s throne.', 11);
command:

-- Lesson 9: Minor Prophets Part 2
(690009, 'Nahum prophesied against:', 'multiple_choice', '["Jerusalem", "Babylon", "Nineveh", "Egypt"]', 'Nineveh', 1),
(690009, 'Habakkuk 2:4 states that "the righteous shall live by":', 'multiple_choice', '["Works", "Faith", "Law", "Sacrifice"]', 'Faith', 2),

(690010, 'The major theme of covenant in the Old Testament finds fulfillment in:', 'multiple_choice', '["The Law of Moses", "The New Covenant in Christ", "The Davidic kingdom", "The temple"]', 'The New Covenant in Christ', 1),
